4 files

Can you design the drainage system?

Of course, let me analyse the project requirements and regulations.

19 steps
⌃
  • Loading project context...
  • Location identified: London, UK.
  • Checking Approved Document H.
  • Checking BS EN 12056-2 for gravity drainage.
  • Checking BS EN 12056-3 for roof drainage.
  • Reviewing local water authority requirements.
  • Loading company standard.
  • Checking similar project approaches.
  • Client preference found: SVPs to be boxed in risers.
  • Finding rooms from Revit model...
  • 24 rooms found requiring drainage.
  • Checking number of sanitary outlets per room.
  • Calculating discharge units per BS EN 12056.
  • Checking for additional drainage requirements.
  • Reading project specification...
  • Calculating condensate connection requirements.
  • Looking for civil engineering pop-up information...
  • ✕Civil data not found in project folder.
  • Stopped — awaiting civil data.

I've analysed the project against Approved Document H and BS EN 12056. Local authority requirements and similar projects checked. Client preference for boxed SVPs noted.

However, I need the civil engineering information to locate the drainage pop-ups. Do you have this available?

Yes, sorry just added to the folder now.

8 steps
⌃
  • Continuing analysis...
  • Reading civil engineering drawings.
  • Parsing drainage pop-up locations from civil data.
  • Cross-referencing with room layout requirements.
  • Pop-up locations differ from optimal positions.
  • Checking if relocation is feasible per Approved Doc H.
  • ✎Placing pop-up markers on drawing for review.+366drainage-layout.rvt
  • Analysis complete.

I've cross-referenced the civil pop-up locations with the room requirements. Some positions differ from optimal — I've marked these for your review.

Ready to proceed with the full drainage design? Type yes or proceed to continue.

Type "yes" or "proceed" to generate drainage layout
∞ Build ▾
Mode
∞ Build Full autonomous design mode - creates and places elements
☰ Designer Designer mode - outlines steps before executing
⚙ QA QA Mode - identifies and fixes issues in existing designs
? Query Query the model for answers and golden thread decision making logic

Model

BUILD MEP.0.1✓Production-ready model optimized for mechanical, electrical and plumbing systems
BUILD A.0.1✓Architectural coordination model for space planning and layout optimization
LLM Mode ⚠

Included in Revit CoPilot, an array of commands for design and modelling.

573+ Commands Exposed Revit commands from the chat — every routine MEP task
561 MCP Tools Context, read, write and action tools for the AI agent
Create your own Commands Build custom commands and workflows from the UI
Connect your own plugins Exposed tool calling — wire your own modules into the Copilot
QA Manager Continuous quality assurance against your project standards
CoBie Manager BIM hand-over data — validated, exported, ready for the client
Document Controller Transmittals, registers, revision tracking from the model
Drawing Production Sheets, views, annotations, titleblocks — automated from chat
AI Model Set up Worksets, levels, grids, templates — project initialisation in one prompt
AI Notes Automated annotation and markup directly on the model
AI Smart Tagging Intelligent element tagging — rooms, equipment, services

Clash Solver

Ingests your ACC clash report, proposes a resolution for every clash, and applies the resolutions you accept — straight into the live model.

Stops reporting clashes. Starts solving them.

Connect Clash Solver to your ACC project. It pulls the latest clash report, deduplicates against the previous run, and triages: real clash vs ghost (model-link drift, family swap, view-filter mismatch). For each real clash it proposes a resolution — re-route the smaller service, raise the ceiling void in the affected zone, swap a fitting, etc. — and shows you what the model would look like after.

You accept, refine, or reject each proposal. Accepted resolutions are applied to the live model as auditable Revit transactions (one-click undo). Rejected ones go into a 'why we kept this' log so the next sync doesn't propose the same thing.

Open Clash Solver →

CoBie Manager

Continuously populates and validates the COBie spreadsheet against the live Revit model. Every cell traceable to its source element.

A live COBie deliverable, never a panic before hand-over.

CoBie Manager is the COBie tool every BIM coordinator wishes Revit shipped with. Populates 20+ COBie sheets from the live model, validates against BS 1192-4 and your project EIR, and tracks every change with an audit log.

Every cell traces back to the source Revit element. No manual mapping, no copy-paste from schedules — the agent reads the model, fills the workbook, flags what's missing, and hands you a file ready for the client.

Open CoBie Manager →

Templates you own

Create your own templates — sell your own templates. Two engines that turn your standards into repeatable deliverables.

REPORT

Every recurring report — composed and traceable to source.

Report Builder turns the most repetitive part of an engineer's week into a five-minute review. Every report stays versioned, every claim is traceable to the source data — model, calcs, photos, minutes — and the formatting matches your house style.

Eight templates ship in the box: site report, MEP strategy, option appraisal, IESVE report, clash report, Stage 2 submission, Stage 3 submission, survey writeup. Point any template at a project and it pulls the model views, calc outputs, meeting minutes and site photos — and assembles the draft.

Open Report Builder →

The MEP specification, drafted from your live model.

Specbuilder is the tool every MEP practice has tried to build in-house and given up on. It cross-references your live Revit model against the brief, applies your house standards, layers in your preferred manufacturer data, and writes a stage-appropriate specification.

Lock sections you've hand-edited, regenerate only what's changed downstream. NBS, CIBSE, ASHRAE or your own house template. Outputs to Word, PDF, or your DMS in one click.

Open Specbuilder →

Agentic services

Managed services where Adelphos runs the agent against your business — finances, project management, document control. Plus four email services for one-off work.

Agentic module

AI Finances

Automated bookkeeping, invoicing and cash-flow forecasting — driven by your live project data.

Automated Reconciliation Matches invoices to POs and flags discrepancies within minutes
Cash-flow Forecasting Rolling 12-month forecast tied to your programme milestones
Management Accounts Monthly packs generated from live data — no spreadsheet assembly
Request a walkthrough → Agentic module

AI Project Management

Programme tracking, risk registers and reporting — the agent keeps the project on rails.

Programme Tracking Live Gantt sync — flags slippage the day it happens, not at the next review
Risk Register Agent-maintained register with auto-scored likelihood and impact
Status Reporting Weekly and monthly reports drafted from live data — ready for sign-off
Request a walkthrough → Agentic module

AI Document Controller

Transmittals, registers, revision tracking — the agent runs your document control.

Transmittal Processing Reads incoming transmittals and logs them to the register automatically
Revision Tracking Tracks every revision with full history and approval status
Approval Chasing Agent sends reminders for outstanding approvals on a configurable schedule
Request a walkthrough →

Just email us — we handle it.

Four agent-run services that work over email. Forward the brief or the model, receive the deliverable.

COBie Generator Forward the model, receive a validated COBie deliverable.
cobie@adelphos.ai
Revit Modelling Agent Email a markup, receive the Revit model update.
model@adelphos.ai
Schematic Designer Describe the system, receive a production-ready schematic.
schematics@adelphos.ai
Specification Writer Forward a brief, receive a complete MEP specification section.
specs@adelphos.ai

Calculation engines

Verified calc modules the agent calls during design. Each engine runs server-side and returns auditable results.

SAP EPC SBEM Cable Calculations Lighting Calculations Thermal Calculations Pipe & Duct Sizing
Read about the calc layer →

Integrates with

The agent reads from and writes to the tools you already use. The platform plugs into your existing Revit, AutoCAD, Excel, Word, IESVE and AutoCAD MEP files — no migration required.

No data stored. No chase-ups. No spam. Uploaded data lives on AWS / Azure infrastructure you control — we don't see it. Your models, your documents, your calculations stay yours.

View security note →

Try the Revit Copilot.

Most of the platform is self-serve — the agentic services need a 30-minute walk-through.

Download the Revit add-in Talk to us
